author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm C

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Ye heard the Psalm, brethren, while it was being chanted: it is short, and not obscure: as if I had given you an assurance, that ye should not fear fatigue.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The title of this Psalm is, “A Psalm of confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The verses are few, but big with great subjects; may the seed bring forth within your hearts, the barn be prepared for the Lord’s harvest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Jubilate,” therefore, “unto the Lord, all ye lands” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Psalm giveth this exhortation to us, that we jubilate unto the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor doth it, as it were, exhort one particular corner of the earth, or one habitation or congregation of men; but since it is aware that it hath sown blessings on every side, on every side it doth exact jubilance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Doth all the earth at this moment hear my voice?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet the whole earth hath heard this voice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All the earth is already jubilant in the Lord; and what is not as yet jubilant, will be so.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For blessing, extending on every side, when the Church was commencing to spread from Jerusalem throughout all nations, everywhere overturneth ungodliness, and everywhere buildeth up piety: the good are mingled with the wicked throughout all lands.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Every land is full of the discontented murmurs of the wicked, and of the jubilance of the good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then is it, “to jubilate”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the title of the present Psalm especially maketh us give good heed to this word, for it is entitled, “A Psalm of confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, to jubilate with confession?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is the sentiment thus expressed in another Psalm: “Blessed is the people that understandeth jubilance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Surely that which being understood maketh blessed is something great.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: May therefore the Lord our God, who maketh men blessed, grant me to understand what to say, and grant you to understand what ye hear: “Blessed is the people that understandeth jubilance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let us therefore run unto this blessing, let us understand jubilance, let us not pour it forth without understanding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what use is it to be jubilant and obey this Psalm, when it saith, “Jubilate unto the Lord, all ye lands,” and not to understand what jubilance is, so that our voice only may be jubilant, our heart not so?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the understanding is the utterance of the heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am about to say what ye know.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: One who jubilates, uttereth not words, but it is a certain sound of joy without words: for it is the expression of a mind poured forth in joy, expressing, as far as it is able, the affection, but not compassing the feeling.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A man rejoicing in his own exultation, after certain words which cannot be uttered or understood, bursteth forth into sounds of exultation without words, so that it seemeth that he indeed doth rejoice with his voice itself, but as if filled with excessive joy cannot express in words the subject of that joy.…Those who are engaged at work in the fields are most given to jubilate; reapers, or vintagers, or those who gather any of the fruits of the earth, delighted with the abundant produce, and rejoicing in the very richness and exuberance of the soil, sing in exultation; and among the songs which they utter in words, they put in certain cries without words in the exultation of a rejoicing mind; and this is what is meant by jubilating.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then are we jubilant?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When we praise that which cannot be uttered.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For we observe the whole creation, the earth and the sea, and all things that therein are: we observe that each have their sources and causes, the power of production, the order of birth, the limit of duration, the end in decease, that successive ages run on without any confusion, that the stars roll, as it seemeth, from the East to the West, and complete the courses of the years: we see how the months are measured, how the hours extend; and in all these things a certain invisible element, I know not what, but some principle of unity, which is termed spirit or soul, present in all living things, urging them to the pursuit of pleasure and the avoidance of pain, and the preservation of their own safety; that man also hath somewhat in common with the Angels of God; not with cattle, such as life, hearing, sight, and so forth; but somewhat which can understand God, which peculiarly doth belong to the mind, which can distinguish justice and injustice, as the eye discerneth white from black.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In all this consideration of creation, which I have run over as I could, let the soul ask itself: Who created all these things?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who made them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who made among them thyself?…I have observed the whole creation, as far as I could.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I have observed the bodily creation in heaven and on earth, and the spiritual in myself who am speaking, who animate my limbs, who exert voice, who move the tongue, who pronounce words, and distinguish sensations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when can I comprehend myself in myself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then can I comprehend what is above myself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet the sight of God is promised to the human heart, and a certain operation of purifying the heart is enjoined; this is the counsel of Scripture.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Provide the means of seeing what thou lovest, before thou try to see it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For unto whom is it not sweet to hear of God and His Name, except to the ungodly, who is far removed, separated from Him?…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Be therefore like Him in piety, and earnest in meditation: for “the invisible things of Him are clearly seen, being understood by the things that are made;” look upon the things that are made, admire them, seek their author.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If thou art unlike, thou wilt turn back; if like, thou wilt rejoice.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when, being like Him, thou shalt have begun to approach Him, and to feel God, the more love increaseth in thee, since God is love, thou wilt perceive somewhat which thou wast trying to say, and yet couldest not say.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Before thou didst feel God, thou didst think that thou couldest express God; thou beginnest to feel Him, and then feelest that what thou dost feel thou canst not express.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when thou hast herein found that what thou dost feel cannot be expressed, wilt thou be mute, wilt thou not praise God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wilt thou then be silent in the praises of God, and wilt thou not offer up thanksgivings unto Him who hath willed to make Himself known unto thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou didst praise Him when thou wast seeking, wilt thou be silent when thou hast found Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By no means; thou wilt not be ungrateful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Honour is due to Him, reverence is due to Him, great praise is due to Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Consider thyself, see what thou art: earth and ashes; look who it is hath deserved to see, and What; consider who thou art, What to see, a man to see God!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I recognise not the man’s deserving, but the mercy of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Praise therefore Him who hath mercy.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Serve the Lord with gladness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: All servitude is full of bitterness: all who are bound to a lot of servitude both are slaves, and discontented.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not the servitude of that Lord: there will be no groaning there, no discontent, no indignation; no one seeketh to be sold to another master, since it is a sweet service, because we are all redeemed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Great happiness, brethren, it is, to be a slave in that great house, although in bonds.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not, bound slave, confess unto the Lord: ascribe thy bonds to thine own deservings; confess in thy chains, if thou art desirous they be changed into ornaments.…At the same time thou art slave, and free; slave, because thou art created such; free, because thou art loved by God, by whom thou wast created: yea, free indeed, because thou lovest Him by whom thou wast made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Serve not with discontent; for thy murmurs do not tend to release thee from serving, but to make thee a wicked servant.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou art a slave of the Lord, thou art a freedman of the Lord: seek not so to be emancipated as to depart from the house of Him who frees thee.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will, therefore, saith he, live separate with a few good men: why should I live in common with crowds?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Well: those very few good men, from what crowds have they been strained out?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If however these few are all good: it is, nevertheless, a good and praiseworthy design in man, to be with such as have chosen a quiet life; distant from the bustle of the people, from noisy crowds, from the great waves of life, they are as if in harbour.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Is there therefore here that joy? that jubilant gladness which is promised?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not as yet; but still groans, still the anxiety of temptations.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even the harbour hath an entrance somewhere or other; if it had not, no ship could enter it; it must therefore be open on some side: but at times on this open side the wind rusheth in; and where there are no rocks, ships dashed together shatter one another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where then is security, if not even in harbour?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And yet it must be confessed, it is true, that persons in harbour are in their degree much better off than when afloat on the main.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them love one another, as ships in harbour, let them be bound together happily; let them not dash against one another: let absolute equality be preserved there, constancy in love; and when perchance the wind rusheth in from the open side, let there be careful piloting there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Now what will one who perchance presideth over such places, nay, who serveth his brethren, in what are called monasteries, tell me?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I will be cautious: I will admit no wicked man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How wilt thou admit no evil one?…Those who are about to enter, do not know themselves; how much less dost thou know them?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For many have promised themselves that they were about to fulfil that holy life, which has all things in common, where no man calleth anything his own, who have one soul and one heart in God: they have been put into the furnace, and have cracked.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How then knowest thou him who is unknown even to himself?…Where then is security?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here nowhere; in this life nowhere, except solely in the hope of the promise of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But there, when we shall reach thereunto, is complete security, when the gates are shut, and the bars of the gates of Jerusalem made fast; there is truly full jubilance, and great delight.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Only do not thou feel secure in praising any sort of life: “judge no man blessed before his death.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: By this means men are deceived, so that they either do not undertake, or rashly attempt, a better life; because, when they choose to praise, they praise without mention of the evil that is mixed with the good: and those who choose to blame, do so with so envious and perverse a mind, as to shut their eyes to the good, and exaggerate only the evils which either actually exist there, or are imagined.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus it happeneth, that when any profession hath been ill, that is, incautiously, praised, if it hath invited men by its own reputation, they who betake themselves thither discover some such as they did not believe to be there; and offended by the wicked recoil from the good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Brethren, apply this teaching to your life, and hear in such a manner that ye may live.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Church of God, to speak generally, is magnified: Christians, and Christians alone, are called great, the Catholic (Church) is magnified; all love each other; each and all do all they can for one another; they give themselves up to prayers, fastings, hymns; throughout the whole world, with peaceful unanimity God is praised.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Some one perhaps heareth this, who is ignorant that nothing is said of the wicked who are mingled with them; he cometh, invited by these praises, findeth bad men mixed with them, who were not mentioned to him before he came; he is offended by false Christians, he flieth from true Christians.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Again, men who hate and slander them, precipitately blame them: asking, what sort of men are Christians?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are Christians?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Covetous men, usurers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Are not the very persons who fill the Churches on holidays the same who during the games and other spectacles fill the theatres and amphitheatres?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They are drunken, gluttonous, envious, slanderers of each other.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: There are such, but not such only.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this slanderer in his blindness saith nothing of the good: and that praiser in his want of caution is silent about the bad.…Thus also in that common life of brethren, which exists in a monastery: great and holy men live therein, with daily hymns, prayers, praises of God; their occupation is reading; they labour with their own hands, and by this means support themselves; they seek nothing covetously; whatever is brought in for them by pious brethren, they use with contentedness and charity; no one claimeth as his own what another hath not; all love, all forbear one another mutually.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast praised them; thou hast praised; he who knoweth not what is going on within, who knoweth not how, when the wind entereth, ships even in harbour dash against one another, entereth as if in hope of security, expecting to find no man to forbear; he findeth there evil brethren, who could not have been found evil, if they had not been admitted (and they must be at first tolerated, lest they should perchance reform; nor can they easily be excluded, unless they have first been endured): and becometh himself impatient beyond endurance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who asked me here?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I thought that love was here.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And irritated by the perversity of some few men, since he hath not persevered in fulfilling his vow, he becometh a deserter of so holy a design, and guilty of a vow he hath never discharged.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And then, when he hath gone forth himself too, he also becometh a reproacher, and a slanderer; and records those things only (sometimes real), which he asserts that he could not have endured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But the real troubles of the wicked ought to be endured for the society of the good.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Scripture saith unto him: “Woe unto those that have lost patience.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what is more, he belcheth abroad the evil savour of his indignation, as a means to deter them who are about to enter; because, when he had entered himself, he could not persevere.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of what sort are they?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Envious, quarrelsome, men who forbear no man, covetous; saying, He did this there, and he did that there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wicked one, why art thou silent about the good!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou sayest enough of those whom thou couldest not endure: thou sayest nothing of those who endured thy wickedness.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “O serve the Lord with gladness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 2): he addresseth you, whoever ye are who endure all things in love, and rejoice in hope.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Serve the Lord,” not in the bitterness of murmuring, but in the “gladness of love.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come before His presence with rejoicing.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is easy to rejoice outwardly: rejoice before the presence of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not the tongue be too joyful: let the conscience be joyful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Come before His presence with a song.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Be ye sure that the Lord He is God” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who knoweth not that the Lord, He is God?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But He speaketh of the Lord, whom men thought not God: “Be ye sure that the Lord He is God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let not that Lord become vile in your sight: ye have crucified Him, scourged Him, spit upon Him, crowned Him with thorns, clothed Him in a dress of infamy, hung Him upon the Cross, pierced Him with nails, wounded Him with a spear, placed guards at His tomb; He is God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “It is He that hath made us, and not we ourselves.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is He that hath made us: “and without Him was not anything made that was made.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What reason have ye for exultation, what reason have ye for pride?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Another made you; the Same who made you, suffereth from you.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But ye extol yourselves, and glory in yourselves, as if ye were created by yourselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is good for you that He who made you, make you perfect.…“We are His people, and the sheep of His pasture.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Sheep and one sheep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These sheep are one sheep: and how loving a Shepherd we have!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He left the ninety and nine, and descended to seek the one, He bringeth it back on His own shoulders ransomed by His own blood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Shepherd dieth without fear for the sheep, who on His resurrection regaineth His sheep.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Enter into His gates with confession” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: At the gates is the beginning: begin with confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thence is the Psalm entitled, “A Psalm of Confession:” there be joyful.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confess that ye were not made by yourselves, praise Him by whom ye were made.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let thy good come from Him, in departing from whom thou hast caused thine evil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Enter into His gates with confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the flock enter into the gates: let it not remain outside, a prey for wolves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how is it to enter?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let the gate, that is, the commencement for thee, be confession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whence it is said in another Psalm, “Begin unto the Lord with confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What he there calleth “Begin,” here he calleth “Gates.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Enter into His gates in confession.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And when we have entered, shall we not still confess?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Always confess Him: thou hast always what to confess for.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is hard in this life for a man to be so far changed, that no cause for censure be discoverable in him: thou must needs blame thyself, lest He who shall condemn blame thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore even when thou hast entered His courts, then also confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When will there be no longer confession of sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In that rest, in that likeness to the Angels.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But consider what I have said: there will there be no confession of sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I said not, there will be no confession: for there will be confession of praise.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt ever confess, that He is God, thou a creature; that He is thy Protector, thyself protected.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In Him thou shalt be as it were hid.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Go into His courts with hymns; and confess unto Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Confess in the gates; and when ye have entered the courts, confess with hymns.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hymn are praises.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Blame thyself, when thou art entering; when thou hast entered, praise Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Open me the gates of righteousness,” he saith in another Psalm, “that I may go into them, and confess unto the Lord.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Did he say, when I have entered, I will no longer confess?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even after his entrance, he will confess.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what sins did our Lord Jesus Christ confess, when He said, “I confess unto Thee, O Father”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He confessed in praising Him, not in accusing Himself.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Speak good of His Name.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For the Lord is pleasant” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Think not that ye faint in praising Him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Your praise of Him is like food: the more ye praise Him, the more ye acquire strength, and He whom ye praise becometh the more sweet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His mercy is everlasting.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For He will not cease to be merciful, after He hath freed thee: it belongeth to His mercy to protect thee even unto eternal life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His mercy,” therefore, “is to everlasting: and His truth from generation to generation” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Understand by “from generation to generation,” either every generation, or in two generations, the one earthly, the other heavenly.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here there is one generation which produceth mortals; another which maketh such as are everlasting.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His Truth is both here, and there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Imagine not that His truth is not here, if His truth were not here, he would not say in another Psalm: “Truth is risen out of the earth;” nor would Truth Itself say, Lo, I am with you alway, even unto the end of the world.”
